questions.text: Which one of the following sentences contains an opinion?
questions.subject: English

options.explanation: Fish live in water: This is a fact that can be objectively verified through scientific observation.

options.text: Chocolate is the best ice-cream flavour.
options.explanation: An opinion expresses a personal belief or judgment. While some people might love chocolate ice cream the most, it's a matter of preference, not an objective fact. There's no single "best" flavour, as taste is subjective.

options.text: Polar Bears live in the Arctic.
options.explanation: Polar Bears live in the Arctic: This is another fact supported by scientific evidence.

options.text: Ants are much smaller than elephants.
options.explanation: Ants are much smaller than elephants: This is a statement based on measurable size differences, which can be objectively observed.

questions.explanation: An opinion expresses a personal belief or judgment. While some people might love chocolate ice cream the most, it's a matter of preference, not an objective fact. There's no single "best" flavor, as taste is subjective.

questions.text: Read the given passages and answer any SIX questions including Question No. (i) appended to it. Question No. (i) about summary writing is compulsory carrying 08 marks while the rest of the questions carry 04 marks each.Islam has attached extraordinary value and importance to youths and the young age. The Rasool (BP) had a high esteem for youths. ‘He (2%) valued them and reposed a strong confidence in them. In the preparation for the battle of Uhud, for instance, The Rasool (BY), in reverence to the passions and emotions of the youth went even against his own opinion and decided to face the enemy outside the city in an open field. The Rasoolx (BP) ) spent his youth in a dignified and sublime way. Even before his accession to the position of prophethood, he (ae) never indulged himself in vices that were prevalent in those days. During his youth, his nobility of soul, purity of heart, his strict adherence to truth and honesty and his stern sense of duty earned him titles of ‘Al Amin’- the trustworthy-and, Al-Sadiq-the true. Thus, he (RP) set an example for the youth of the coming generations to follow the path of rectitude in all circumstances and always to lead a pious and pure life. In his personal life, the Rasool was the embodiment of modesty and chastity, emphasizing the same upon his followers. He did not let it go even in his very private life. He declared ‘Haya’ to be the integral part of faith. Once, dwelling upon the significance of modesty (Haya), he said as narrated by Abu Mas’ud, “If you do not feel ashamed then do whatever you like”. A chaste, modest and civilized Muslim youth is the ideal of the teachings of Hazrat Muhammad (BP) It has been made binding upon all to obey the beloved Rasool (BY) in their lives. Since complete obedience is impossible without having a thorough knowledge of Seerat e Tayyeba, hence, it becomes mandatory for the Muslim youth to acquaint themselves with the Seerat e Tayyeba. The youth, being the backbone of the body of Ummah, must follow the teachings of the beloved Rasool (BP) in letter and Spirit to attain purity in their character.Question:Write down the summary of the passage and suggest a suitable title.How is the youth given self-esteem by the beloved Rasool (BHF)?How did the Rasool (Els spend his youth?What is the Rasool’s (Be) saying about modesty (Haya)?What is the importance of modesty in the life of youth.What are the duties of youth nowadays in the light of the given paragraph?Why should we follow the teachings of Hazrat Muhammad (BP) i in our life?Write the meanings of any four of underlined words.
questions.subject: English

questions.text: Read the following poetic extract carefully and answer the questions appended to it:(i) If you can talk with crowds and keep your virtue,Or walk with Kings — nor lose the common touch,\f neither foes nor loving friends can hurt you,If all men count with you, but none too much;If you can fill the unforgiving minuteWith sixty seconds’ worth of distance run,Yours is the Earth and everything that’s in it,And — which is more — you Il be a Man, my son!QUESTIONS:(i) Explain the line, “or walk with king — nor lose the common touch’.(ii) What is the significance of the word, “If”?(iii) Write down the theme of the given stanza.(OR)(II) Somebody said that it couldn’t be done,But, he with a chuckle repliedThat “may be it couldn't’ but he would be oneWho wouldn't say so till he’d tried.So he buckled right in with the trace of a grinOn his face. If he worried he hid it.He started to sing as he tackled the thingThat couldn’t be done, as he did it.QUESTIONS:(i) What is the attitude of the people towards the difficult task as narrated by the poet?(ii) How does the person in the stanza react towards the attitude of the people?(iii) Write down the theme of the given stanza.
questions.subject: English
